sae_features.label: Extracellular LRR recognition surface
sae_features.description: Extracellular leucine-rich repeat (LRR) ectodomains—most strongly the N‑terminal capping segment (LRRNT) and the first few LRR repeats that include solvent‑exposed loop/turn positions forming the recognition surface—in cell‑surface and secreted recognition proteins across eukaryotes; occasional weak tagging of non‑LRR extracellular N‑termini

sae_features.label: LRR Asn-ladder turn
sae_features.description: Conserved “Asn‑ladder”/turn position within leucine‑rich repeats (LRRs): the canonical Asn at the NxL (or N/CxL) slot on the concave face turn of each repeat, or the equivalent structural position in degenerate repeats and immediately adjacent inter‑repeat loops.

sae_features.label: LRR convex non-β elements
sae_features.description: A structural signal for leucine‑rich repeat (LRR) domains, specifically the convex‑face non‑β elements: the recurring turns/loops and short helices at the C‑terminal side of each repeat and in inter‑repeat/capping segments. The feature highlights the periodic, Leu‑rich repeat architecture across diverse proteins, is largely silent on the concave β‑strand segments of LRRs and on non‑LRR domains, and often captures N‑linked glycosylation sequons when present in extracellular LRR ectodomains.

sae_features.label: LRR beta strand hydrophobics
sae_features.description: Conserved hydrophobic β-strand positions in leucine‑rich repeat (LRR) domains—the strand‑core Leu/Val/Ile/Met that recurs once per repeat (the L of the canonical LxxLxLxxN/C motif) forming the concave parallel β‑sheet of the LRR solenoid; minor spillover to the immediate strand–loop boundary

sae_features.label: Solenoid Asn ladder motif
sae_features.description: Consensus “Asn ladder” position of solenoid repeat domains—chiefly LRRs, but also analogous right-handed beta-helix repeats seen in some carbohydrate-active enzymes and phage adhesins—i.e., the conserved Asn/Thr/Cys (or Asx ladder) in the loop/turn following the repeat’s beta-strand, with secondary sensitivity to the adjacent leucines, yielding periodic activation across multi-repeat arrays.

sae_features.label: LRR strand-helix junction loops
sae_features.description: Short, solvent‑exposed loop/turn at the β‑strand→α‑helix junction within individual leucine‑rich repeat (LRR) motifs—the recurring convex‑side inter‑repeat connector of LRR domains, often enriched in Gly/Ser/Thr/Asp/Glu and sometimes encompassing coil→helix starts and nearby glycosylation sites. The feature is specific to LRR regions and is independent of the proteins’ other domains or functions.

sae_features.label: C-terminal GPI-like sorting propeptides
sae_features.description: Cleavable C-terminal sorting propeptides in eukaryotic secreted/cell-surface proteins—most commonly the GPI-anchoring signal immediately downstream of the omega site—characterized by an S/T/A/G-rich spacer and often (in classic GPI cases) a short hydrophobic, helical tail recognized by GPI-transamidase; similar low-complexity C-terminal propeptides without a TM-like tail can also be captured.

sae_features.label: LRR concave beta-strand/loop
sae_features.description: Concave β-strand–loop units of leucine-rich repeat (LRR) domains, including their N/C-terminal capping segments; the feature detects the hydrophobic core residues at the start of each repeat (L/I/V/A in the LxxLxLxxN consensus) together with adjacent loop positions enriched for Asn/Ser/Thr/Cys, across diverse LRR-containing proteins.
